political.s: FedEx has demonstrated consistent right-leaning political donations through its PAC, with 100% of disclosed contributions going to Republican candidates. Total documented PAC/lobbying contributions indicate substantial investment in right-aligned political advocacy aligned with the logistics and business interests.

charity.s: <cite index="15-1">In FY24, FedEx contributed $55 million in total charitable contributions around the world.</cite> The company focuses on disaster relief, sustainable transportation, and community development through its FedEx Cares program, with emphasis on healthcare access, disaster response, and women in STEM fields.
charity.focus: Disaster relief, healthcare access, sustainable transportation, women in STEM, community development

environment.s: <cite index="4-1">In its most recent GRI report, FedEx scored 85 out of 100 in environmental impact, surpassing the industry average of 70.</cite> <cite index="9-2">From Fiscal Year 2009 through Fiscal Year 2022, FedEx reduced the intensity of its CO2e emissions by nearly 50% (on a revenue basis).</cite> The company has committed to carbon-neutral operations by 2040 with significant investments in vehicle electrification.

labor.s: FedEx has faced multiple OSHA violations and safety citations across its operations. <cite index="49-1">FedEx has accumulated $704,629,025 in penalty totals since 2000 across 354 violation records.</cite> Issues include hazardous materials handling violations, worker safety compliance gaps, and ongoing labor disputes regarding contractor classification and wages.

dei.s: FedEx previously maintained strong DEI commitments for nearly 50 years with programs including diversity recruitment, mentorship initiatives, and supplier diversity. However, <cite index="22-2,22-26">FedEx took down at least five key DEI web pages some time in February or March 2025.</cite> This recent removal of DEI-focused content suggests a shifting corporate stance.

animals.s: As a logistics and courier company, FedEx's operations do not directly involve animal testing. However, the company has participated in animal transport services including charitable animal rescue operations.

guns.s: FedEx is a logistics company that transports packages and goods. The company does not manufacture or sell firearms as part of its core business operations, though it may transport legally permitted shipments as part of general courier services.

privacy.s: <cite index="51-1">Personal Data must be protected using technical, managerial and physical security measures against the risk of loss or unauthorized access, destruction, use, modification or disclosure.</cite> FedEx maintains global privacy standards and requires consent-based processing, though the company transfers data internationally and uses third-party advertising partners which presents mixed privacy practices.

execPay.s: <cite index="32-1">Rajesh Subramaniam President and Chief Executive Officer received a total compensation of $12.87 M in 2025.</cite> While one source cited an older 416:1 pay ratio, current data suggests significant executive compensation disparity relative to median employee wages in the delivery and logistics sector.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
